steps.title: Expand the product f(x) · g(x)
steps.content:
To find $b$, we expand $(x^2 + bx)(9x^2 - 27x)$ using the distributive property:
$$\begin{align}(x^2 + bx)(9x^2 - 27x) &= x^2(9x^2 - 27x) + bx(9x^2 - 27x)\\&= 9x^4 - 27x^3 + 9bx^3 - 27bx^2\\&= 9x^4 + (9b - 27)x^3 - 27bx^2\end{align}$$

steps.title: Compare coefficients with the given product
steps.content:
Since $f(x)\cdot g(x)=9x^4-26x^3-3x^2$, the coefficient of $x^3$ in our expansion must equal $-26$:
$$9b-27=-26$$
$$9b=1$$
$$b=\frac{1}{9}$$
A Desmos regression check with several nonzero $x$-values gives the same $b$. That matches choice C, so C is correct.
